name: Riley v. California
summary: Police generally may not search digital information on cell phone seized from person arrested without warrant; digital data creates heightened privacy concerns requiring warrant protection.
holding: Police must generally obtain warrant before searching digital information on cell phone, even when phone lawfully seized incident to arrest; search incident to arrest exception does not apply to digital data.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes heightened protection for digital information; critical for analyzing government access to digital records, databases, and electronic communications.
procedural_posture: State criminal convictions reversed and remanded
